label: Pronominal verbs: farcela, andarsene and friends
description: The verbs whose clitics are part of the word: farcela (to manage), andarsene (to leave), cavarsela (to get by), prendersela (to take offence), volerci (to take/require). The learner-facing topic home for their meaning and conjugation; the clitic mechanics themselves live in the pronoun tree and are cited, not re-owned.

label: Conjugating them
description: The three families by their frozen clitic: -sela verbs, -sene verbs, and the ci verbs.

label: -sela verbs: me la cavo, ce l'ho fatta
description: Cavarsela, prendersela, sentirsela, godersela, and farcela's past: the reflexive part conjugates (me, te, se, ce, ve, se) while la stays frozen, and in compound tenses the participle is invariably feminine because it agrees with la: se l'è cavata, ce l'abbiamo fatta.

attributes.common_miss: The participle agreeing with the subject instead of la ('ce l'ho fatto', 'se l'è cavato'), and the reflexive part left unconjugated ('si la cava' for 'me la cavo').

label: -sene verbs: me ne vado
description: Andarsene, starsene, fregarsene: reflexive + ne, with the vowel change (mi diventa me) and essere in compound tenses, participle agreeing with the subject: se n'è andata. The clitic mechanics live in the pronoun tree's ne buckets.

attributes.common_miss: 'Mi ne vado' (the unshifted vowel), and avere in the compound ('se n'ha andato').
canonical_examples: Me ne sto a casa stasera.

label: Ci verbs: ci vuole, ci metto, ce l'ho
description: Volerci (agreeing with what is required: ci vuole un'ora, ci vogliono due ore), metterci (personal subject: ci metto un'ora), entrarci (non c'entra niente), tenerci, avercela con, vederci and sentirci. Ci becomes ce before la/lo/ne. The clitic mechanics live in the pronoun tree's locative-ci idiom buckets.

attributes.common_miss: Volerci left singular with a plural requirement ('ci vuole due ore'), and 'ci l'ho' for 'ce l'ho'.

label: What they mean
description: The meanings are lexical and mostly untransparent; recognising and choosing them is a separate skill from conjugating them.

label: The core set, recognised
description: Farcela = to manage; andarsene = to leave; cavarsela = to get by; prendersela = to take offence; sentirsela = to feel up to it; avercela con = to have it in for; entrarci = to have to do with it. Reading these correctly in context is high-value comprehension from B1 on.

attributes.common_miss: Literal readings: 'non ce la faccio' parsed as making something, 'ce l'ha con me' as possession.
canonical_examples: Non me la sento di guidare stanotte.

label: Volerci or metterci?
description: Both say how long things take, differently: volerci is impersonal and agrees with the time required (ci vogliono due ore per arrivare); metterci has a personal subject (io ci metto due ore). Swapping frames is the classic B1 miss.

attributes.common_miss: 'Io ci voglio due ore' and 'ci mette due ore per arrivare' with no personal subject in view.
canonical_examples: Ci vogliono due ore per arrivare a Roma.
Io ci metto due ore con il traffico.

label: Ce l'ho: the have-it answer
description: Everyday possession answers demand the ce: Hai una penna? Sì, ce l'ho. Bare 'l'ho' sounds unfinished and 'ho' alone answers nothing. Frozen, extremely frequent, and taught nowhere near as early as it is needed.

attributes.common_miss: 'Sì, l'ho' and 'sì, ho': the missing ce, plus 'ci l'ho' for 'ce l'ho'.
canonical_examples: Hai una penna? Sì, ce l'ho.
